causal_chain.to: Water molecules gain kinetic energy when heated
causal_chain.mechanism: Increased temperature provides energy for molecular motion

causal_chain.from: Water molecules gain kinetic energy when heated
causal_chain.mechanism: Sufficient kinetic energy allows molecules to break free from liquid state

causal_chain.to: Water transitions from liquid to gaseous state (boiling)
causal_chain.mechanism: Phase change occurs when intermolecular forces are overcome

causal_chain.to: Boiling point of water is 100 degrees Celsius
causal_chain.mechanism: At standard atmospheric pressure, the temperature at which the vapor pressure of water equals the atmospheric pressure is 100°C
